business.headline: Are memes and reels turning stock markets into a casino for investors?Billionaire investor Warren Buffett recently said in a podcast that he no longer fully understands parts of today's market and even likened it to a casino for investors. IndiaToday.in spoke to experts to understand whether markets are increasingly being driven by speculation rather than fundamentals.

8th Pay Commission: Why employees unions want salary hikes every 5 yearsAs part of its continuing exclusive coverage of the 8th Pay Commission discussions, IndiaToday.in has learnt that one of the major suggestions raised during these meetings was to reduce the gap between two Pay Commissions from 10 years to 5 years.

Sensex, Nifty open higher as oil drops on Trump's Iran peace signalBenchmark indices opened higher on Wednesday as lower oil prices and earnings-led moves boosted sentiment. The rise came with caution, as analysts flagged credibility concerns around Iran-related signals and stretched broader-market valuations.

G7 in talks to set up permanent body to oversee critical minerals supplyG7 countries are discussing a permanent secretariat to carry forward critical minerals initiatives beyond rotating presidencies. The talks lay bare differences over stockpiling and leadership as members try to reduce reliance on China.

Ethanol gets price protection, maize doesn't: What's going wrong?The government has set the MSP for maize at Rs 2,400 per quintal. However, in mandis, farmers are often forced to sell at much lower rates.

Cognizant eyes up to 15,000 job cuts, India likely to see biggest impactThe possible job cuts come at a time when many large IT firms are restructuring. Slower spending by clients, rising automation, and the growing use of AI are changing the way companies operate.

Zee files case against Nykaa for alleged misuse of songs, seeks $210,000 damagesZee Entertainment has filed a lawsuit in the Delhi High Court, seeking damages of $210,000. The company claims that Nykaa used several of its copyrighted songs in Instagram reels to promote products to its large online audience.

Sensex, Nifty close lower as record-low rupee, oil worries weigh on marketsThe BSE Sensex fell 251.61 points, or 0.33%, to close at 77,017.79, while the Nifty 50 declined 86.50 points, or 0.36%, to settle at 24,032.80.

Gratuity rises under new labour rules, but what if you quit before 5 years?Gratuity is a lump-sum benefit paid when your employment ends, and most people believe you must complete a minimum of five years to claim it. But that's not entirely accurate.

2% DA hike explained: Only Rs 600 more on a Rs 30,000 basic pay?A 2% Dearness Allowance (DA) hike may sound like good news, but the actual increase in your salary is fairly modest. On a Rs 30,000 basic pay, it adds just Rs 600 a month, so what's behind this small hike?
Biocon founder Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw names niece Claire Mazumdar as successorClaire Mazumdar is currently the founder and CEO of Bicara Therapeutics, a company that was incubated by Biocon.

Sensex, Nifty down: Why is stock market falling today?Markets had opened weak and extended losses through the morning, indicating that the election-led optimism seen in the previous session was short-lived.

Sensex, Nifty bounced back in April. What can investors expect in May?After a broad-based April rally, can markets sustain momentum in May as earnings take centre stage, separating fundamentally strong companies from momentum-driven stocks while global risks continue to cap sharp upside?

Sensex, Nifty slip at open as oil spike and weak rupee unsettle marketsThe BSE Sensex was down 170.40 points, or 0.22%, at 77,099.00 at 9:35 am, while the Nifty 50 fell 65.85 points, or 0.27%, to 24,053.45 as of 9:35 am.

Why Warren Buffett says he doesn't understand today's markets any moreAce investor and former CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, Warren Buffett, said today's market is harder to read, with trading increasingly driven by short-term bets.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will market fall amid weak rupee, elevated oil prices?Markets are headed for a cautious start as weak global cues, elevated oil prices and a soft rupee weigh on sentiment. Election results and quarterly earnings may still shape intraday moves across stocks.

OnEMI Technology IPO crosses 5x subscription on Day 3. Check latest GMPThe allotment of shares is expected to be finalised on May 6, 2026. The company is likely to list on the NSE and BSE on May 8, 2026.
NSE introduces electronic gold receipts to digitise gold tradingEach EGR is backed by real gold, ensuring that investors still own physical value, just without the need to store it themselves.

Govt shields fuel buyers, rules out relief for oil PSUs amid crude spike: ReportThe government has ruled out compensating state-run fuel retailers while keeping retail petrol, diesel and LPG prices steady. The move shifts part of the cost to bulk and industrial users as inflation concerns stay in focus.

FIFA World Cup 2026 faces broadcast uncertainty in India, ChinaIn India, a Reliance-Disney joint venture has offered $20 million for 2026 World Cup broadcast rights, a fraction of FIFA's ask, which was not acceptable to soccer's global governing body, two sources told Reuters on Monday.

entertainment.headline: Did Vijay's son Jason Sanjay unfollow him after poll win? Truth behind claim Thalapathy Vijay's son Jason Sanjay came under scrutiny after social media users said he did not follow his father on Instagram. The claim spread alongside Vijay's electoral win in Tamil Nadu.

Ektaa Kapoor backs Naagin 7 editor after viral goof-up Ektaa Kapoor reacted to Naagin 7's viral blue screen error by introducing and backing her long-time editor. Her posts turned the trolling into a light-hearted defence of a trusted collaborator.

Dhurandhar's Rakesh Bedi 'kills it' with Nora Fatehi on dance set. Video Rakesh Bedi shared an on-set video as he shot a dance track with Nora Fatehi. Their light-hearted exchange comes amid renewed attention on his fan-favourite turn in Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Prabhas's Fauzi shoot paused after crew member dies in Hyderabad road accident The shoot of Fauzi, starring Prabhas, has been halted after a road accident near Hyderabad left one crew member dead and five others injured. The incident comes as the film nears completion.

Author Jenny Han asks fans to stay away from The Summer I Turned Pretty film set Prime Video and Jenny Han have asked fans to stop sharing the location of The Summer I Turned Pretty film set and turning up during filming. They said crowding and online posts are disrupting production and creating safety concerns for the cast and crew.

Raja Shivaji box office Day 5: Steady weekday hold for Riteish's film, eyes Sairat Raja Shivaji has shown a strong hold over the weekdays as it manages to remain steady on both Monday and Tuesday. The Riteish Deshmukh film may just dethrone Sairat to become the top Marathi grosser soon.

Palaash Muchhal faces FIR over caste abuse allegations by Smriti Mandhana's friend Vidnyan Mane has filed an Atrocities Act case against singer-composer Palaash Muchhal over an alleged unpaid of Rs 25 lakh film investment and caste-based abuse. The case comes months after Palash's cancelled wedding with cricketer Smriti Mandhana.

SRK is a large part of my career repertoire: KJo gets emotional at Met Gala Karan Johar reflected on Shah Rukh Khan's role in his career as he made his Met Gala debut in 2026. The filmmaker shared how the moment defined his bond with the superstar and with SRK's Met debut in 2025, KJo believes it's full circle now.

Kamal Haasan, Nani lead tributes after producer RB Choudary dies in car accident Kamal Haasan, Nani and several film personalities paid tribute after producer RB Choudary died in a car accident on Tuesday. Their messages remembered his support for younger artistes as the Tamil and Telugu industries mourned his sudden death.

Michael Jackson tops global music charts, 17 years after his death Michael Jackson has been listed in the No. 1 position in global streaming ranking after the release of the biopic Michael. The film released in theatres on April 24.

Angelina Jolie wins key ruling in French winery legal battle with Brad Pitt A Los Angeles court has said Angelina Jolie need not disclose private emails in the Chateau Miraval fight with Brad Pitt. The ruling keeps 22 messages out of the case for now, though Pitt can raise the issue again.

SA Chandrasekhar story: Man who built Vijay, parted ways, then hailed TVK's big win As TVK's win became clear, SA Chandrasekhar publicly praised son Vijay's decision to contest Tamil Nadu polls alone. It was a full-circle moment for a father-son story of mentorship, estrangement and reconciliation.

Ranbir-Alia join Kapoor family to support Neetu's Daadi Ki Shaadi screening Ranbir Kapoor and Alia Bhatt joined the Kapoor family at the screening of Neetu Kapoor's Daadi Ki Shaadi. The gathering underscored the film's family-centred theme ahead of its May 8, 2026 release.

Ameesha returns to Mumbai after being stuck in Muscat amid UAE airspace closure Ameesha Patel returned to Mumbai after her New York flight was diverted to Muscat during the UAE airspace closure. Her posts captured a long airport wait as fresh missile attacks disrupted travel plans.

Christopher Nolan reviews The Devil Wears Prada 2; calls it a fabulous film Christopher Nolan revealed that he watched The Devil Wears Prada 2 and found it terrific. He also reviewed Emily Blunt's performance in the sequel.

Ranveer Singh ideal for Shaktimaan: Mukesh Khanna's co-actor Surendra Pal Actor Surendra Pal, Mukesh Khanna's co-star in Shaktimaan the TV series, said Ranveer Singh would be the ideal choice to star in a film version of the show. Pal urged Khanna to reconsider his decision about not casting the Dhurandhar star.

How Viggo Mortensen reacted to Jamie Dornan's Aragorn casting in The Hunt for Gollum Director Andy Serkis shared Viggo Mortensen's reaction to Jamie Dornan taking over as Aragorn in The Lord of the Rings: The Hunt for Gollum.

Kriti Sanon deserved National Award for Mimi, film changed my career: Sai Tamhankar Sai Tamhankar said Mimi marked a turning point in her career and changed how the industry saw her. She also praised Kriti Sanon's National Award win.

Keralam wrap: Akshay Kumar shares fun pic from set of film with Vidya, Raashii Akshay Kumar wrapped the Kerala schedule of Anees Bazmee's untitled film and shared a set picture with Vidya Balan and Raashii Khanna. The update underlines his reunion with Vidya and marks his return to Bazmee's direction after 15 years.

general news.headline: Top Vijay aide meets EPS. Is AIADMK set to back TVK?Sources said that a faction led by AIADMK Rajya Sabha MP CV Shanmugam is considering backing Vijay's TVK, who emerged the single-largest party in Tamil Nadu, winning 108 out of 234 Assembly seats.

Live: Karti Chidambaram confirms Congress backing for Vijay as TVK leader meets AIADMK chiefAssembly Election Result Live Updates: High-stakes deliberations are underway in four states and one Union Territory where the Assembly election results were just announced. After BJP's historic win in West Bengal, the political temperature is at its peak, with an intense face-off between BJP and the Trinamool Congress, alongside reports of violence. In Tamil Nadu, following a surprise performance by Vijay, discussions on finalising a coalition are continuing, with decisive moves expected today as Congress extends its support to him. In Kerala, Congress is set to begin internal deliberations to choose a new Chief Minister, with central observers reaching the state today. In Assam and Puducherry, where the NDA has secured victories, attention has now shifted to government formation. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates on post-poll developments from across the states.

SA Chandrasekhar story: Man who built Vijay, parted ways, then hailed TVK's big winAs TVK's win became clear, SA Chandrasekhar publicly praised son Vijay's decision to contest Tamil Nadu polls alone. It was a full-circle moment for a father-son story of mentorship, estrangement and reconciliation.

Mamata stalled Teesta pact: Bangladesh ruling party sees hope in BJP Bengal winBangladesh's ruling party congratulated the BJP on its Bengal victory, praising Suvendu Adhikari's leadership. It said the result could improve ties and revive the Teesta agreement, blaming the Mamata Banerjee government for delays and expressing hope the project would now move forward under BJP rule.

How Vijay is sparking fear in Tamil Nadu, by not speakingTVK leader Vijay's poll campaign in Tamil Nadu was defined by near silence. The actor-turned-politician said little, but the TVK won 108 seats (out of 234 seats). Vijay's quiet run has continued even after winning the election. Consequently, we do not know what Vijay brings to the office of CM.
Unfortunate: Karti Chidambaram on DMK leader's backstabber remark over TVKUnfortunate, doesn't get post-poll coalitions: Karti Chidambaram on DMK leader's backstabber remark over TVK

3 dead in arson, clashes in post-poll Bengal; EC directs cops to take strict action LIVEPost-poll violence in West Bengal continued for a second day, with killings, arson and vandalism reported across several districts. The clashes triggered rival accusations, police deployment and an Election Commission order for zero tolerance.

Did Vijay predict his future? 'TN07 CM 2026' number plate from GOAT fuels buzzA clip from Vijay's 2024 film GOAT, showing his vehicle bearing 'TN 07 CM 2026' number plate, is circulating widely on social media following his party's historic electoral debut in Tamil Nadu.

Play of the day: Axar's captaincy disaster class costs DC 2 points vs CSKIPL 2026: Axar Patel's toss call and batting-order gambles unravelled as Delhi Capitals lost to Chennai Super Kings in Delhi. The defeat sharpened scrutiny on Delhi's middle order and on a captain still searching for certainty.

Live: Bengal BJP chief says 2 workers killed, party showing restraintBengal Election 2026 Live Updates: Violence has marred West Bengal's post-election atmosphere, with reports of arson, vandalism, and murder emerging from multiple parts of the state despite a decisive electoral outcome. Clashes and unrest were reported from areas including Kolkata, Birbhum, Howrah, and Baranagar, with political parties trading accusations. The Trinamool Congress (TMC) alleged that BJP cadres hacked one of its workers to death in Birbhum's Nanoor, while police reported vandalism of party offices in several locations. Viral visuals on social media showed mobs armed with sticks chasing and assaulting individuals, with allegations of targeted attacks on TMC supporters and intimidation of local businesses. Tensions escalated in central Kolkata after a TMC office near Hogg Market was allegedly demolished, triggering panic among traders. Amid the unrest, Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar directed strict action against those involved in violence. He ordered continuous patrols and enforced a zero-tolerance policy, warning that anyone inciting or engaging in violence would be arrested immediately. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

Palaash Muchhal faces FIR over caste abuse allegations by Smriti Mandhana's friendVidnyan Mane has filed an Atrocities Act case against singer-composer Palaash Muchhal over an alleged unpaid of Rs 25 lakh film investment and caste-based abuse. The case comes months after Palash's cancelled wedding with cricketer Smriti Mandhana.

Opposition Mukt Bharat? Verdict 2026 and the BJP's expanding hegemonyVerdict 2026 has reinforced the BJP's national dominance even as Tamil Nadu and Kerala bucked the wider trend. The results have sharpened questions over whether India is moving towards an opposition-free political order.
Chirag Shetty and team celebrate Thomas Cup bronze with viral Kavkaz trend reelAfter India won bronze at the Thomas Cup 2026, Chirag Shetty shared a Kavkaz trend reel featuring the team.

Anthropic creates 10 new AI agents for bank and finance workAnthropic has launched 10 ready-to-run AI agents for banks, insurers and other finance firms. The release has sharpened debate over entry-level jobs even as the company says users remain in the loop.

Dumped, DMK brands Congress backstabber over TVK supportAs per sources, Congress passed a resolution in a virtual meeting on Tuesday night to extend conditional support to TVK, seeking cabinet posts and chairmanships in certain boards, with a formal announcement expected on Thursday, followed by a meeting with Vijay in Panaiyur.

Punjab on alert after blast outside Jalandhar BSF HQ, Amritsar camp targetedA scooter exploded and caught fire near the BSF headquarters in Jalandhar, and hours later, another blast was reported outside a BSF camp in Amritsar in the border state of Punjab.

Too tall for duty? 6.6-ft Telangana bus conductor given alternate role after neck painA 6-foot-tall bus conductor was reassigned out-of-designation duty after his height made bus work physically difficult. The order has drawn attention to workplace adaptability and ergonomic support in public transport jobs.

Regime of infiltrators in Haryana: Rahul Gandhi renews 'vote theft' attack on BJPRahul Gandhi renewed his 'vote theft' attack on BJP, claiming electoral manipulations helped the party win seats and even governments.

Isro team confirms Sun is making older, dead satellites crash on EarthAn Isro-led study found that stronger solar activity makes low Earth orbit debris lose altitude faster. The finding could affect satellite fuel planning and collision risk in crowded orbits.

Guess I called it: Pooja Hegde drops old video from Jana Nayagan set after Vijay winAn Instagram post that shows Pooja Hegde gleefully declaring she had rightly called her Jana Nayagan co-star Vijay's election win has gone viral. In a separate X post, she congratulated Vijay, saying it was 'party time' now.

57-year-old Lucknow man appears for NEET 2026, revisits a long-delayed goalA 57-year-old Lucknow resident appeared for NEET after returning to studies decades later. His attempt highlights how some older aspirants are re-entering formal education through competitive exams.

WHO traces 88 flight passengers who travelled with Hantavirus-infected fliersWHO is tracing passengers on a Saint Helena to Johannesburg flight after two co-travellers died of hantavirus. The effort follows concerns that fellow fliers and others on the journey may have been exposed.

Why Iran is striking UAE amid ceasefireThe UAE's status as a regional economic powerhouse, coupled with its close strategic ties to the United States and Israel, has made it a prime target of Iran, leaving the Gulf's most globally connected hub among the hardest hit by Iranian strikes.

India is known for its spices. Climate change is killing its flavoursThe harsh truth is that Indian food, one of the most celebrated and adored cuisines around the world, is at risk. And only time will tell whether India's indigenous flavour bombs can prevail against climate change.

Ranbir Kapoor snaps as paparazzi crowd him, Alia at Daadi Ki Shaadi screeningRanbir Kapoor asked photographers to step back after they crowded him and Alia Bhatt at a Mumbai screening of Daadi Ki Shaadi. Videos from the exhange have surfaced online.

Riteish Deshmukh says children should watch Raja Shivaji to know real historyRiteish Deshmukh said Raja Shivaji is bringing families to theatres and performing strongly at the box office. He also urged children to watch the film to better understand history beyond textbooks.

sports.headline: Why Kartik Sharma fits perfectly in CSK's long-term plans? Ex-IND star explains Sanjay Bangar believes Kartik Sharma has found the perfect home at CSK, praising the franchise's backing, security and long-term vision after the youngster's recent IPL 2026 rise.

Akeal Hosein forgotten no more: At CSK, the Powerplay King has found his kingdom IPL 2026, DC vs CSK: Akeal Hosein spent years being world-class and ignored by IPL franchises. Chennai Super Kings took the punt. Now, the Powerplay King is making them look very, very smart.

Sanju Samson is CSK's backbone: Ruturaj Gaikwad grateful after star opener sinks DC IPL 2026: Sanju Samson struck an unbeaten 87 as Chennai Super Kings beat Delhi Capitals by eight wickets in Delhi. The win kept CSK in the playoff race and prompted Ruturaj Gaikwad to call him the backbone of the batting line-up.

Momentum is key: PBKS, SRH look to peak in last leg of group-stage games IPL 2026: Punjab Kings travel to Hyderabad to play their treverse fixture against the SRH. Both the teams will be searching for momentum as they make a final charge to secure the playoffs places.

How can DC be a serious cricket team? Ex-India star slams team after CSK loss Dodda Ganesh questioned Delhi Capitals' selections and tactics after the defeat to Chennai Super Kings. The criticism has intensified scrutiny of the side's combinations, captaincy and fading play-off push.

Arsenal into Champions League final as Arteta hails 'historic' Emirates night Arsenal are back in the Champions League final after 20 years. Bukayo Saka's goal and a disciplined display against Atletico Madrid sealed a historic night for Mikel Arteta's side at the Emirates.

Play of the day: Axar's captaincy disaster class costs DC 2 points vs CSK IPL 2026: Axar Patel's toss call and batting-order gambles unravelled as Delhi Capitals lost to Chennai Super Kings in Delhi. The defeat sharpened scrutiny on Delhi's middle order and on a captain still searching for certainty.

Home (dis)advantage? DC rue unpredictable pitches after 4 losses in 5 games DC vs CSK: Delhi Capitals have now lost 4 out of their 5 home games in the IPL 2026. After their latest loss against CSK, Badani rued the fact that the team has not really been able to understand what the pitch is going to offer on a given match-day.

Messi's India tour promoter breaks silence, threatens to sue TMC for 100 crore The organiser of Lionel Messi's GOAT tour - Satadru Dutta - has hit back at TMC right after the party was ousted from Bengal. Dutta has threatened to sue the party for Rs 100 crore.

Fleming 2-0 Badani: DC coach trolled after CSK clean sweep Delhi in IPL 2026 Delhi Capitals coach Hemang Badani faced heavy trolling after Chennai Super Kings clean swept the franchise on Tuesday, in IPL 2026. Back in 2024, Badani had taken digs as CSK head coach Stephen Fleming.

Aryna Sabalenka threatens to boycott French Open over prize money dispute Aryna Sabalenka has warned that players could boycott the French Open if the organisers do not increase the prize pool in 2026. Despite the strong stance, Sabalenka expressed hope that negotiations would lead to a resolution.

Didn't want to be selfish: Selfless Samson places team above hundred vs DCIPL 2026: Sanju Samson anchored Chennai Super Kings's chase with an unbeaten 87, prioritising the team's win over a personal hundred against Delhi Capitals. Supported by Karthik Sharma in a match-winning stand, CSK secured a comfortable victory to stay in playoff contention.

Sanju Samson powers CSK's double over Delhi to add spice to Chennai numbers gameIPL 2026, DC vs CSK: Sanju Samson struck an unbeaten 87 to help Chennai Super Kings successfully chase 156 in Delhi on Tuesday and complete a double over the Capitals. With 10 points from as many matches, the Super Kings have joined the season of numbers game in Chennai. 118 for TVK and a possible 16 for the Super Kings is all many down South will be hoping for.

Vinesh Phogat lands in trouble: Wrestler flagged for missing dope testVinesh Phogat has been handed her first whereabouts failure by the International Testing Agency after missing an out-of-competition dope test in December 2025. While not an anti-doping violation, the warning puts her one step closer to potential sanctions under global testing rules.

Pegged back by back-to-back losses, Punjab Kings hunt momentum vs SRHAfter staying unbeaten for more than half of the IPL 2026, Punjab Kings have lost back to back matches. As they take on SRH in their 10th game of the season, assistant coach Brad Haddin hopes that Punjab find much-needed momentum going into the playoffs stage.

IPL tickets scam busted by Hyderabad Police after SRH vs KKR gameHyderabad police arrested three men for black-marketing complimentary IPL tickets during the SRH-KKR match at Uppal. Investigators said forged VIP letterheads were used to obtain passes, exposing a racket around match access.

DC vs CSK: Fit-again Lungi Ngidi returns after mandatory concussion layoffIPL 2026, DC vs CSK: Lungi Ngidi has returned to the Delhi Capitals playing XI after completing his mandatory concussion lay-off, providing a timely boost to the team's pace attack ahead of the clash against Chennai Super Kings.

Does BCCI control ICC? South Africa spinner makes massive allegationSimon Harmer said India's financial muscle gives the BCCI decisive sway over cricket's direction. His remarks came as South Africa's recent rise challenged old hierarchies on and off the field.

Thomas Cup bronze, no buzz: Chirag, Prannoy seek more recognitionChirag Shetty and HS Prannoy said India's Thomas Cup bronze returned with little recognition at home. They said the muted response makes it harder for players to stay motivated for team commitments.

Riyan Parag's elevation as Rajasthan captain 'makes no sense': Sanjay ManjrekarFormer India batter Sanjay Manjrekar has strongly criticised Rajasthan Royals for appointing Riyan Parag as the franchise's full-time captain for IPL 2026, saying the decision "makes no sense" from a cricketing perspective.

Bengaluru unlikely to host IPL final over MLA ticket issueIPL 2026: BCCI is reconsidering Bengaluru as host for the IPL 2026 play-offs and final amid a political row over MLA tickets. The uncertainty revives questions over venue management as RCB remain firmly in contention.

Ex-India cricketer quits TMC day after BJP sweep, slams outgoing sports ministerBengal's junior sports minister Manoj Tiwary has quit TMC just day after BJP's sweep in Bengal elections. Tiwary levels serious allegations against outgoing sports minister Aroop Biswas.

Arsenal shift focus from Premier League race to Atletico clash in UCL semi-finalArsenal and Atletico Madrid head into their Champions League semi-final second leg at the Emirates after a 1-1 draw in Madrid and reports of fireworks outside Atletico's London hotel. The tie pits Arsenal's control and defensive structure against Atletico's disruptive style, with the result to be decided over 90 minutes.

Virat Kohli told me I'll fit perfectly: Karthik opens up about RCB coaching roleDinesh Karthik said Virat Kohli's backing helped him accept Royal Challengers Bengaluru's batting coach and mentor role after retirement. The move strengthened RCB's support staff and preceded their IPL title.

MI need to look after Bumrah: Ravi Shastri decodes pacer's poor IPL 2026 formFormer India coach Ravi Shastri blamed mental fatigue for Jasprit Bumrah's poor IPL 2026 form, saying the Mumbai Indians must carefully manage the workload of their pace spearhead.

Relief from Indian captaincy lifted Rohit Sharma's batting: Gavaskar after MI beat LSGIPL 2026: Sunil Gavaskar said Rohit Sharma's batting has grown since the captaincy burden was lifted. The remark came after Rohit's 84 off 44 led Mumbai Indians past Lucknow Super Giants.

I was never rated as a T20 player: Rahul glad to silence IPL strike-rate chatterKL Rahul admitted that things have not always gone his way in T20 cricket, but said he is proud to have broken the perception of being only a Test specialist after years of criticism over his strike rate and white-ball game.

No one really cares: Sat-Chi's viral cryptic post after Thomas Cup medal winSatwik Rankirajreddy and Chirag Shetty shared a cryptic Instagram story after India's Thomas Cup bronze. The post drew attention to the muted reception around the team's campaign and return home.

Gold medal in hand, no way home: Pole vault prodigy Kavinraja left strandedPole vaulter Kavinraja S was stranded at Rajahmundry railway station for over 12 hours while returning from Bhubaneswar with his poles. The episode highlighted the travel difficulties athletes face while carrying specialised equipment.

Ollie Robinson sets sights on England Test recall after positive McCullum feedbackEngland pacer Ollie Robinson said encouraging messages from head coach Brendon McCullum and managing director Rob Key have revived his hopes of returning to England's Test side.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
